title: tetracera sarmentosa subsp. andamanica
intro: Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. andamanica is a subspecies of the Tetracera sarmentosa plant, commonly found in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, characterized by its woody stem and fragrant flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its use in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. andamanica include its ability to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, with herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions being used to harness its medicinal properties.
